children.name: Theraphosa blondi
children.description: World's largest spider by mass (up to 170g, 30cm leg span). Native to Venezuelan and Brazilian rainforests. Diet mainly invertebrates, frogs, and small rodents. Stridulation audible 5m away.

children.name: Brachypelma hamorii
children.description: Iconic tarantula of western Mexico with vivid orange-red leg-joint patches. CITES II listed due to overcollection. Females may live 30 years.

children.name: Brachypelma auratum
children.description: Similar to hamorii with flame-like orange banding; restricted to Guerrero state, Mexico.

children.name: Poecilotheria regalis
children.description: Arboreal tarantula of peninsular India. Striking geometric pattern. Fast and defensive; IUCN Endangered.

children.name: Poecilotheria metallica
children.description: Critically Endangered; known from a single forest patch near Nandyal, India. Extraordinary metallic blue colouration.

children.name: Chromatopelma cyaneopubescens
children.description: Native to arid scrubland of northern Venezuela. Adults develop metallic blue carapace, orange abdomen, green legs -- arguably the most colourful spider on Earth.

children.name: Selenocosmia stirlingi
children.description: Large arid-zone tarantula documented to prey on frogs, small lizards, and juvenile snakes. Venom causes prolonged illness in dogs.
